---
title: LRZ Dry-Run Templates
phase: thesis
audience: developer
status: current
owner: docs
---

# LRZ Dry-Run Templates {#lrz-dry-run-templates}

This page defines the documentary LRZ dry-run surface for scaling ARIA-NBV
oracle generation, rollout generation, VIN training, and diagnostics. It is not
a setup guide and does not implement any generation code. The goal is to make
the cluster submission shape, DSS staging layout, shard ownership, resume
behavior, and atomic-write expectations reviewable before large jobs exist.

The template files live under `scripts/templates/lrz/`. The companion planning
notes live under `.configs/lrz/`. Both surfaces use placeholders for current
ARIA entry points because the implementation command names must be inspected
from the active checkout before real jobs are created.

## Template Index

| Workflow | Template | Launch shape | Large output root |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Oracle generation | `scripts/templates/lrz/oracle_generation_dry_run.sbatch` | Slurm array, Pyxis container, one GPU per shard | `$ARIA_DSS/caches/oracle/staging/$RUN_ID/shards/` |
| Rollout generation | `scripts/templates/lrz/rollout_generation_dry_run.sbatch` | Slurm array, Pyxis container, one GPU per shard | `$ARIA_DSS/data/staging/rollouts/$RUN_ID/shards/` |
| VIN training | `scripts/templates/lrz/vin_training_dry_run.sbatch` | One-node multi-GPU Pyxis container | `$ARIA_DSS/checkpoints/vin/$RUN_ID/` |
| Diagnostics | `scripts/templates/lrz/diagnostics_dry_run.sbatch` | CPU or GPU Slurm array, Pyxis container | `$ARIA_DSS/logs/diagnostics/$RUN_ID/` |

Each template prints the `srun --container-image ... --container-mounts ...`
shape that Pyxis would use with an Enroot image URI such as
`nvcr.io#nvidia/pytorch:24.10-py3`. The printed command includes `--dry-run`
and placeholder entry points, so copying the structure into a real job requires
an explicit implementation pass.

## DSS Layout

Use `$ARIA_DSS` as the root for large artifacts and keep `$HOME` for source,
small config, shell setup, SSH/git config, and private credentials. The dry-run
planning layout is:

```text
$ARIA_DSS/
  data/raw/
    ase/
    atek/
  data/processed/<dataset_version>/
  data/staging/
    manifests/
    rollouts/<run_id>/
      shards/
      _tmp/
      manifests/
  caches/oracle/
    staging/<run_id>/
      shards/
      _tmp/
      manifests/
  caches/vin/<offline_store_version>/
  checkpoints/vin/<run_id>/
  logs/
    slurm/
    diagnostics/<run_id>/
    wandb/
  containers/
  tmp/<slurm_job_id>/
  .cache/{uv,pip,huggingface,torch}/
```

This layout is a staging convention for LRZ planning, not a package storage
schema. It intentionally keeps future rollout staging under `data/staging/`
until the rollout dataset/reporting contract is finalized.

## Quotas And Inodes

Before any scale submission, inspect the active DSS allocation with
`dssusrinfo all` and record both byte quota and inode pressure in the run notes.
Do not hard-code quota or inode numbers in templates because LRZ allocations and
project containers can differ.

The generated artifacts should be shard-oriented. Prefer archive shards,
HDF5/Zarr-style stores, WebDataset-style shards, or compact JSONL manifests over
millions of loose files. Package caches, model caches, temporary extraction, and
intermediate writer state belong under `$ARIA_DSS/tmp/` or `$ARIA_DSS/.cache/`,
not under `$HOME`.

## Shard Ownership

One Slurm array task owns one final shard path. The shard ID should come from
`SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID` plus a deterministic manifest, not from broad directory
scans. Two jobs must never write the same final shard path.

Every final shard should carry owner metadata, for example `_owner.json`, with
the run ID, Slurm job ID, array task ID, template name, source dataset or cache
version, git commit, and config hash. Final manifests should reference shard IDs
and content hashes rather than temp paths.

## Resume

A shard is complete only when its final completion marker exists and validates.
Recommended marker names are `_SUCCESS.json` for generated shards and a
workflow-specific marker for dry-run training reports. Resume code should skip
validated completed shards, retry failed shards explicitly, and leave stale temp
directories for operator review or deliberate cleanup.

Failed attempts should write a `_FAILED.json` sidecar or a failed-manifest entry
outside the completed shard root. Large retry jobs should be launched from a
manifest of missing or failed shard IDs rather than by re-scanning the whole DSS
tree.

## Atomic Writes

Heavy outputs should be written to a temp directory on the same DSS filesystem,
validated, and then promoted into the final path by rename. The completion
marker is written last. Final shards and immutable VIN offline stores must not
be mutated in place; if a writer cannot update atomically, create a new
versioned shard and update the manifest only after validation.

VIN training jobs should treat the offline store as read-only. Checkpoints may
be resumed from explicit paths under `$ARIA_DSS/checkpoints/vin/$RUN_ID/`, but
`latest` or `best` pointers should be updated only after the checkpoint file is
fully written.

## Pre-Flight Checks

Before turning a dry-run template into a real job:

1. Inspect current ARIA console scripts from the LRZ checkout.
2. Replace placeholder entry points and arguments in a copied template.
3. Confirm `ARIA_DSS`, `ARIA_REPO`, `LRZ_CONTAINER_IMAGE`, and the shard
   manifest.
4. Run a one-shard smoke first, then scale arrays only after the output marker,
   owner sidecar, and resume behavior are verified.
5. Keep the real command under review until the package writer contract exists.
